title: Ensure VPC changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure VPC changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is possible to have more than one VPC within an account; additionally, it is also possible to create a peer connection between two VPCs, enabling network traffic to route between them. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes made to VPCs.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. VPCs in AWS are logically isolated virtual networks that can be used to launch AWS resources. Monitoring changes to VPC configurations will help ensure that VPC traffic flow is not negatively impacted. Changes to VPCs can affect network accessibility from the public internet and additionally impact VPC traffic flow to and from the resources launched in the VPC.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = CreateVpc) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpc) || ($.eventName = ModifyVpcAttribute) || ($.eventName = AcceptVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = CreateVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = RejectVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = AttachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DetachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DisableVpcClassicLink) || ($.eventName = EnableVpcClassicLink) }" 6. Note the <vpc-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <vpc-changes-metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<vpc-changes-metric>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for VPC changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <vpc-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<vpc-changes-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateVpc) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpc) || ($.eventName = ModifyVpcAttribute) || ($.eventName = AcceptVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = CreateVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = RejectVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = AttachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DetachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DisableVpcClassicLink) || ($.eventName = EnableVpcClassicLink) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <vpc-changes-alarm> --metric-name <vpc-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail logs VPC events (e.g., CreateVpc, DeleteVpc, CreateVpcPeeringConnection), but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist. These changes are recorded but not actively monitored unless configured.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_vpc_changes_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
This automated program will verify the correct monitoring of VPC changes through CloudTrail and CloudWatch services, following these steps:

1. **List CloudTrail Trails:**
   - Use AWS CLI command: `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` or the Boto3 method `client.describe_trails()`.
   - **Validation:** Identify trails where the property `IsMultiRegionTrail` is set to `true`.

2. **Get Trail Status:**
   - Use AWS CLI command: `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>` or the Boto3 method `client.get_trail_status(Name=<trail-name>)`.
   - **Validation:** Ensure `IsLogging` is `TRUE`.

3. **Verify Event Selectors:**
   - Use AWS CLI command: `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>` or the Boto3 method `client.get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail-name>)`.
   - **Validation:** Check that at least one event selector is present with `IncludeManagementEvents` set to `true` and `ReadWriteType` set to `All`.

4. **List Metric Filters:**
   - Use AWS CLI command: `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>` or the Boto3 method `client.describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail-log-group_name>)`.
   - **Validation:** Ensure that the output contains a metric filter that matches the defined filter pattern for VPC changes.

5. **List CloudWatch Alarms:**
   - Use AWS CLI command: `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<vpc-changes-metric>]'` or the Boto3 method `client.describe_alarms()`.
   - **Validation:** Check that at least one alarm is associated with the `vpc-changes-metric`.

6. **Verify SNS Subscription:**
   - Use AWS CLI command: `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>` or the Boto3 method `client.list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)`.
   - **Validation:** Ensure there is at least one active subscription, checking that `SubscriptionArn` contains a valid ARN.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- The program will pass if all checks yield the expected configurations (e.g., CloudTrail is active and multi-region, appropriate metric filters for VPC changes exist, alarms are set up, and SNS subscriptions are present).
- The program will fail if any condition is not met, indicating that monitoring of VPC changes is not properly configured.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as "Automated" is correct. The tasks outlined can be effectively automated using AWS APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI commands. AWS provides comprehensive support for monitoring and managing CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS configurations programmatically.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The AWS CLI commands and boto3 methods listed are valid and appropriate for the tasks described. However, there are some areas where additional clarity or steps could enhance the robustness of the audit:
  - The description of the `describe-alarms` command/query could be more precise by specifying the `Namespace` to ensure it targets the correct metric.
  - The program name `aws_cloudtrail_vpc_changes_monitoring_enabled` is somewhat descriptive but could be more specific about the scope, such as including "real_time" or "metric_filter" to indicate the nature of the monitoring.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Metric Filter Validation:** The description of the metric filter should include the specific pattern used to detect VPC changes. This ensures clarity on what constitutes a "VPC change" in the context of the audit.
- **Alarm Validation:** It would be beneficial to specify that the alarm should trigger on specific conditions, such as a threshold of VPC changes within a certain period.
- **SNS Subscription:** Consider verifying that the SNS topic is configured to notify the appropriate security team or system, not just that a subscription exists.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** A more descriptive program name could be `aws_cloudtrail_vpc_changes_real_time_monitoring_enabled`.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and leverages AWS capabilities effectively. The suggested improvements are minor and do not detract from the overall validity of the approach.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Enhance the audit by specifying the metric filter pattern for VPC changes and detailing the conditions under which alarms should trigger. Update the program name for clarity and specificity. These modifications will improve the audit's precision and ensure comprehensive monitoring of VPC changes.
